article_title: NIH Study Reveals Astrocytes Actively Shape Fear Memory in the Amygdala, Challenging Decades of Neuron-Centric Models

content_preview.title: NIH Study Reveals Astrocytes Actively Shape Fear Memory in the Amygdala, Challenging Decades of Neuron-Centric Models
content_preview.summary: A Nature study finds that star-shaped brain cells called astrocytes encode, maintain, and regulate fear memories alongside neurons, opening new paths for PTSD treatment.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Astrocytes, the star-shaped brain cells long dismissed as passive support structures, actively encode and regulate fear memories in the amygdala, according to a [study published in Nature](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-025-10068-0) by researchers at the National Institutes of Health and the University of Arizona. The findings fundamentally challenge decades of neuroscience orthodoxy that placed neurons at the sole center of fear processing and memory formation.

editor_notes.source_verification: ScienceDaily: verified — all researchers, methodology, findings, Halladay quote, Nature DOI confirmed. Nature and PubMed: paywalled/abstract-only but DOI confirmed.

editor_notes.originality: Fresh topic — no prior astrocyte/fear memory coverage.
